_about: Example binding map for the xenodot-twin try-it kit — a compact smart-home digital-twin story over the standard Duplex_A model shipped beside this file (Duplex_A_20110907.ifc). Each row binds a home telemetry tag to a REAL IFC element by its 22-char GlobalId (the twin-import join key). The GlobalIds below are for THIS IFC and are DETERMINISTIC across converts of the same file, so they work as-is against a GLB/sidecar you build from the bundled Duplex_A_20110907.ifc. For any OTHER model, re-derive them: run tools/ifc_convert.py to produce models/<name>_props.json (the sidecar, keyed by GlobalId), then verify each id before authoring — `node -e 'const s=require("./models/duplex_props.json");const id="2O2Fr$t4X7Zf8NOew3FNqI";console.log(s[id]?.ifc_class,"|",s[id]?.name)'` should print the ifc_class + name shown in that row's 'ifc' key. Never trust ids copied from a different model. JSON has no comments, so each row documents itself in 'ifc'/'note'. The sim (tools/sim/server.js --map) derives its tag list and each tag's [min,max] straight from this file, so data and geometry never drift. Ramps: temperature cold->hot = blue #1e63ff -> red #ff2f2f; solar dark->bright = slate #14142a -> amber #ffcf3f; door closed->open = green #37d67a -> red #ff5252. Zone temps paint each zone's EXTERIOR envelope wall, so the whole house reads as a heat map from outside.

bindings.ifc: IfcWallStandardCase / Basic Wall:Exterior - Brick on Block:138157 (ground-floor envelope wall, living-room facade)
bindings.note: Living-room zone temperature in Celsius painted on its exterior wall; 18 C = cool/blue, 30 C = warm/red.

bindings.ifc: IfcWallStandardCase / Basic Wall:Exterior - Brick on Block:138310 (opposite ground-floor envelope wall, kitchen side)
bindings.note: Kitchen zone runs a touch warmer at meal times; same 18-30 C cold->hot ramp.

bindings.ifc: IfcWallStandardCase / Basic Wall:Exterior - Brick on Block:143478 (upper-floor envelope wall, main-bedroom facade)
bindings.note: Main-bedroom zone temperature in Celsius; 18-30 C cold->hot ramp.

bindings.ifc: IfcWallStandardCase / Basic Wall:Foundation - Concrete (417mm):140520 (base wall by the mechanical/utility area — stands in for the boiler)
bindings.note: Boiler flow temperature in Celsius; hotter range (40-80 C) than living zones, same cold->hot ramp so the difference reads at a glance.

bindings.ifc: IfcSlab / Basic Roof:Live Roof over Wood Joist Flat Roof:184483 (the flat roof carrying the PV array)
bindings.note: Rooftop solar output in watts; 0 W = dark slate (no sun), 5000 W = bright amber (full generation).

bindings.ifc: IfcDoor / M_Single-Flush:1250mm x 2010mm:146596 (the widest door, the main entrance)
bindings.note: Front-door open state as a floating Label3D above the door: 0 = closed (green), 1 = open (red). Label response, not a paint, so it reads as a status readout.
